prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for benzaldehyde using DFT at B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation involved three self-consistent-field computations: the neutral ground state (singlet), the cation (doublet, N−1), and the anion (doublet, N+1). Fukui indices were derived from Mulliken partial-charge differences across these three states. The most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻) is the carbonyl oxygen (f⁻ = 0.23840), and the most electrophilic site (highest f⁺) is C-3 on the benzene ring meta to the aldehyde (f⁺ = 0.39939). All three electronic states converged to SCF, and charge conservation checks (sum of f⁺ and sum of f⁻ = 1.0 each) passed without issue. A bar-chart visualization was generated. Geometry was not re-optimized at the DFT level; the provided input coordinates were used as-is. All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true).</prose>
